generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance BuildingIoTSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled sensor deployed across building infrastructure for real-time environmental and operational monitoring, including temperature, humidity, occupancy, and equipment status."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingIoTDataTransmitted
]

instance BimModelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "BIM model integration sensor extracting static geometric and semantic data from Building Information Models (IFC format) for digital twin initialization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BimModelDataTransmitted
]

instance LegacySystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Legacy system interface sensor capturing historical operational data from existing facility management systems and building automation systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LegacySystemDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring sensor collecting vibration, strain, and deformation data from critical building components for integrity assessment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption monitoring sensor tracking real-time power usage across HVAC, lighting, and other building systems for performance optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance BuildingIoTDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingIoTSensor
]

instance BimModelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BimModelSensor
]

instance LegacySystemDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LegacySystemSensor
]

instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric model derived from Building Information Modeling (BIM) representing building structure, components, and spatial relationships in IFC format."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler
]

instance BuildingControlSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavioral model representing building control systems including HVAC, lighting, and security systems with device-specific configurations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlSystemSimulationEnabler
]

instance EnergyPerformanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based energy performance model simulating thermal dynamics, energy consumption patterns, and optimization scenarios for building systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance StructuralHealthModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Finite Element Analysis (FEA) model for structural health monitoring, simulating load distributions and degradation patterns across building components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralAnalysisEnabler
]

instance BuildingIoTData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data from IoT sensors including environmental parameters (temperature, humidity), occupancy status, and equipment operational metrics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BuildingIoTDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataFusionEnabler, RealTimeMonitoringEnabler
]

instance BimModelData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured semantic data extracted from BIM models containing geometric attributes, material properties, and component relationships."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BimModelDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance LegacySystemData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical operational data from legacy facility management systems including maintenance logs, equipment performance records, and past energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LegacySystemDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler, LifecycleManagementEnabler
]

instance StructuralHealthData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structural integrity data including vibration measurements, strain readings, and deformation patterns collected from monitoring sensors."
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralAnalysisEnabler
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series energy consumption data from building systems including HVAC, lighting, and equipment loads with granular temporal resolution."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyAnalyticsEnabler, OptimizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration framework enabling seamless fusion of BIM geometric models with real-time operational data using IFC standard and automated model transformation."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance DataFusionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous data fusion platform combining IoT sensor streams, BIM attributes, and legacy system logs into a unified data model for digital twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance ControlSystemSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine executing physics-based and data-driven models of building control systems (HVAC, lighting, security) for operational optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAndAutomationService, OptimizationService
]

instance EnergyAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Energy performance analytics platform leveraging machine learning and computational fluid dynamics for energy consumption forecasting and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance StructuralAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring and analysis tool using Finite Element Analysis (FEA) to simulate load distributions and predict structural degradation."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, LifecycleManagementService
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data processing pipeline with anomaly detection capabilities for continuous monitoring of building systems and equipment performance."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization platform supporting 3D/4D/5D (time, cost, performance) representations with AR/VR integration for stakeholder collaboration."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics engine utilizing machine learning models for failure prediction, energy optimization, and lifecycle cost estimation."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance OptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization framework applying genetic algorithms and multi-objective optimization techniques for space utilization and energy efficiency improvements."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance LifecycleManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lifecycle management platform supporting as-built model synchronization, adaptive refurbishment planning, and sustainability reporting."
    DTDFVocab:enables LifecycleManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data ingestion and validation service from heterogeneous sources (IoT, BIM, legacy systems) with anomaly detection for equipment failures and energy inefficiencies."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, EquipmentFailureAlertAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Machine learning-driven forecasting service enabling failure prediction, energy consumption optimization, and lifecycle cost estimation through what-if scenario analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, EnergyOptimizationInsight, RefurbishmentRecommendationInsight
]

instance SimulationAndOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Physics-based and data-driven simulation service for design validation, operational optimization, and adaptive refurbishment testing in a digital sandbox environment."
    DTDFVocab:provides MultiPhysicsSimulationInsight, OptimizationAlgorithmInsight, RetrofitStrategyInsight
]

instance ControlAndAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control system integrating digital twin insights with physical actuators for automated adjustments and demand-response integration."
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedAdjustmentAction, DemandResponseAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D/4D/5D visualization service with interactive dashboards supporting AR/VR integration for remote inspections, training, and collaborative reviews."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveMonitoringInsight, ClashDetectionInsight, KpiDashboardInsight
]

instance LifecycleManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "End-to-end digital continuity service from design to decommissioning with as-built model synchronization and adaptive refurbishment planning."
    DTDFVocab:provides AsBuiltSynchronizationInsight, RefurbishmentPlanningInsight, SustainabilityReportingInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-physics optimization service applying genetic algorithms for space utilization and energy efficiency improvements across building systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides SpaceUtilizationInsight, EnergyEfficiencyInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance service for HVAC and structural components using structural health monitoring data and degradation models."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceScheduleInsight, ComponentHealthInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time building performance metrics including structural health, HVAC efficiency, and energy consumption patterns for operational decision-making."
]

instance EquipmentFailureAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert generation for detected equipment failures or operational anomalies requiring immediate attention."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics output identifying potential equipment failures and structural degradation risks based on historical and real-time data."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Energy consumption optimization recommendations including dynamic load balancing and peak shaving strategies for smart grid integration."
]

instance RefurbishmentRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Adaptive refurbishment planning recommendations based on degradation analysis and lifecycle cost estimation models."
]

instance MultiPhysicsSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation results from thermal, structural, and fluid dynamics analyses for design validation and operational optimization."
]

instance OptimizationAlgorithmInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithm outputs for space utilization and energy efficiency improvements across building systems."
]

instance RetrofitStrategyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Tested retrofit strategies evaluated in the digital twin environment before physical implementation to assess performance impacts."
]

instance AutomatedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control actions adjusting HVAC setpoints and other building systems based on occupancy predictions and performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DemandResponseAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Demand-response integration actions enabling grid interaction for peak shaving and energy cost optimization."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ImmersiveMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D/4D/5D visualization of building performance metrics supporting remote inspections and collaborative reviews."
]

instance ClashDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected clashes between building systems and components identified through BIM-DT integration for conflict resolution."
]

instance KpiDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Key performance indicator dashboards tracking energy use intensity, maintenance backlog, and other operational metrics."
]

instance AsBuiltSynchronizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Synchronized as-built models updated with post-construction data for accurate digital twin representation."
]

instance RefurbishmentPlanningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Adaptive refurbishment planning recommendations based on digital twin degradation analysis and performance data."
]

instance SustainabilityReportingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Carbon footprint tracking and sustainability reporting enabled by digital twin energy models and operational data."
]

instance SpaceUtilizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized space utilization recommendations based on occupancy patterns and building performance metrics."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Energy efficiency improvement strategies derived from multi-physics optimization analyses."
]

instance MaintenanceScheduleInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance schedules generated from structural health monitoring and equipment performance data."
]

instance ComponentHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural component health assessments identifying degradation risks and maintenance priorities."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **control flows from digital to physical** via validated commands issued by Facility Managers or automated systems. Command types include **adjustments to HVAC systems, lighting controls, or security protocols**, formatted as structured messages compatible with building automation systems. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are implied to ensure command integrity, though specifics (e.g., cryptographic protocols, access controls) are not detailed. **Feedback systems** provide confirmation of command execution, with bidirectional data exchange ensuring real-time status updates (e.g., acknowledgment of actuator activation). **Conflict resolution** is managed through the DT Framework’s modular architecture, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. The system emphasizes **holistic building life cycle management**, ensuring commands align with operational requirements derived from enriched BIM models."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, implying sub-second to millisecond-level latency for critical operational monitoring and control. The framework supports **dynamic operational needs** through runtime configuration management, enabling adjustments without full redeployment, which suggests adaptable time scales for different services (e.g., sensor data aggregation, analytics, or user interactions). While exact synchronization frequencies or granularity are not specified, the emphasis on **real-time** implies a requirement for low-latency bidirectional data flows, likely leveraging event-driven or streaming protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) to maintain temporal alignment between physical and digital representations. The system’s modular architecture further allows for service-specific time scales, where high-frequency updates (e.g., IoT sensor streams) may coexist with lower-frequency analytical or reporting services."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated constellation architecture** centered around a **modular, service-oriented framework** that orchestrates interactions between physical buildings, BIM models, and operational Digital Twins. Key components include:
  - **DT Framework**: Acts as the central hub, facilitating **bidirectional data exchange** and **holistic life cycle management** through standardized extension points.
  - **Role-Based Orchestration**: Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, and DT Engineers contribute to distinct phases (deployment, monitoring, and extension), with the framework ensuring **runtime configuration management** for adaptability.
  - **Component Integration**: Leverages **enriched BIM models** as the foundational digital representation, transformed into operational Digital Twins via the framework’s pipeline. Integration patterns include **modular service plugins** (e.g., analytics, IoT interfaces) and **runtime adjustments** without full redeployment.
  - **Resource Management**: The system prioritizes **scalability** (from individual components to entire infrastructure systems) and **low-overhead maintainability**, with non-functional requirements addressing interoperability and accessibility across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components (e.g., functional requirements **F_X** in Table III). This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention. This phase ensures seamless transition from static BIM to dynamic DT representations.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, enabling **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts. This phase supports **continuous synchronization** and operational alignment.
  4. **Runtime Operation**: The DT operates with **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control, facilitated by Facility Managers. The **modular architecture** allows integration of custom services and analytics, enabling **evolutionary adaptation** to changing operational needs.
  5. **Extension and Maintenance**: DT Engineers develop and integrate **custom services and plugins** to extend functionality, governed by the framework’s **standardized extension points**. Version control and lifecycle governance are implied through the **modular, extensible architecture**, though explicit methodologies (e.g., versioning protocols) are not detailed in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **fidelity requirements** by enabling **high-precision device modeling** within BIM, where sensors, actuators, and controllers are explicitly defined to reflect their physical counterparts. **Validation and verification** are implicitly supported through the **automated transformation** of BIM models into deployable DT artifacts, ensuring semantic consistency between physical and digital representations. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between static BIM models and dynamic operational data, though the framework mitigates this via **real-time synchronization** mechanisms. **Error detection** is facilitated through **interactive visualization interfaces**, allowing Facility Managers to cross-validate digital states against physical observations. **Quality assurance** processes are embedded in the **modular architecture**, where custom services (e.g., analytics plugins) can enforce domain-specific validation rules. The framework’s reliance on **structured data extraction** from BIM ensures traceability, while **runtime monitoring** capabilities enable continuous fidelity assessment during DT operation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT Framework employs a **modular, interoperable network infrastructure** to support heterogeneous system integration. While specific protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) are not named, the architecture prioritizes **scalability** and **interoperability** across components. Network topology is **centralized**, with the DT Framework acting as a hub for bidirectional data exchange between physical assets and digital representations. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are optimized for real-time synchronization, though exact thresholds are unspecified. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are implied through runtime configuration management, enabling low-overhead updates without redeployment. **Security protocols** for network communication are not explicitly detailed but are inferred to align with non-functional requirements (NF_X) addressing maintainability and accessibility."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system supports **flexible deployment models**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise hosting**, tailored to operational needs. Computational and storage requirements are designed for **scalability**, accommodating individual components or entire infrastructure systems. The framework’s **modular architecture** allows for standardized extension points, enabling custom service integration without full redeployment. **Scalability mechanisms** leverage runtime configuration management, ensuring low-overhead updates. Deployment models are aligned with the DT life cycle, with Building Control Engineers initiating deployment via BIM tools and Facility Managers interacting with operational DTs. **Availability and reliability** are assured through the framework’s systematic approach to bridging static BIM representations with dynamic AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) requirements."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework emphasizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through standardized extension points and **data exchange protocols** compatible with external systems. Key integration aspects include:
  - **BIM Integration**: Enriched BIM models serve as the input for Digital Twin deployment, ensuring alignment with static representations while enabling dynamic operational extensions.
  - **External System Dependencies**: The framework supports integration with **enterprise systems** (e.g., facility management software, IoT platforms) and other Digital Twins via **bidirectional data exchange**, though specific protocols (e.g., IFC, COBie, or proprietary APIs) are not detailed.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: Non-functional requirements explicitly address **cross-system interoperability**, suggesting adherence to industry standards (e.g., OPC UA for IoT, REST/gRPC for service communication) to ensure seamless data flow between physical assets, Digital Twins, and external stakeholders.
  - **Integration Patterns**: The modular architecture allows for **plug-and-play service integration**, enabling DT Engineers to extend capabilities without disrupting existing workflows. Dependencies on external systems are managed through the framework’s **runtime configuration management**, ensuring adaptability to evolving operational needs."
]
